subject: Ecology
title: Rewilding Islands
description: Learn Rewilding Islands in one minute.

hook.text: After removing invasive predators, conservationists reintroduce native species to restored islands.

buildup.text: Seabird colonies return, and their guano fertilizes soil, boosting plant and insect communities.

discovery.text: On New Zealand's offshore islands, rewilding brought back species once thought regionally extinct.

twist.text: Rewilded islands can become seed populations, eventually restocking mainland habitats too.

climax.text: Over 1,200 islands worldwide have been cleared of invasive mammals—the biggest conservation win.

punchline.text: Remove the invader. The island remembers how to heal.

quiz.question: How many islands have been cleared of invasive mammals?
